title: Calendula lip balm
subtitle: A calendula-infused beeswax lip balm in small tins
excerpt: A calendula lip balm made from calendula-infused sweet almond oil, beeswax, and a small amount of shea butter. Anhydrous (no water) so it keeps 12 months without a preservative. The recipe fills six standard 10 g lip balm tins in one session.
sourceNotes: Rosemary Gladstar, Herbal Recipes for Vibrant Health (2008), calendula-infused oil for skin preparations. Bramble Berry lip balm formulation notes (brambleberry.com, May 2026), beeswax percentage range for lip balm versus salve consistency.

content.text: A calendula lip balm. Calendula-infused sweet almond oil provides a light, non-greasy carrier; beeswax at 15% of the batch weight gives the balm its stick consistency; shea butter at 10% adds a softening quality. The result is
and keeps 12 months at room temperature without a preservative.

Prepare the calendula infusion 2-4 weeks before making the balm: fill a clean jar with 75 g sweet almond oil and 1 tablespoon dried calendula petals, seal, and leave in a cool dark cupboard. Strain through muslin before using.

items.name: Sweet almond oil
items.prepNote: calendula-infused sweet almond oil (see prep note above). 83% of the oil phase. Light and absorbs quickly.

items.name: Beeswax pellets
items.prepNote: yellow or white beeswax pellets. 17% of the batch. Sets the consistency; lower beeswax gives a softer balm.

items.name: Shea butter
items.prepNote: refined or unrefined shea butter. Adds a buttery slip to the finished balm.

items.name: Rose geranium essential oil
items.prepNote: optional. Rose geranium or lavender at approximately 1% of the total batch. Add after removing from heat.

content.text: Melt the wax, butter, and oil together

Place the beeswax pellets and shea butter in a heatproof bowl over a saucepan of barely simmering water. Stir gently as the wax melts; it melts above 62°C so allow 5-10 minutes. When the beeswax has fully liquefied, add the calendula-infused sweet almond oil and stir to combine. Remove from the heat.

If using fragrance, add the essential oil at this point and stir to incorporate. Pour the mixture into the tins before it begins to cloud; once beeswax starts to set the batch cannot be poured cleanly. Pour in one smooth motion from the jug into each tin, filling to just below the rim. The balm will shrink slightly as it cools; this is normal. Leave undisturbed for 30 minutes until fully set.

Dab a little on the inside of the wrist and wait 24 hours before using on the lips. Calendula is in the daisy family. If you react to ragweed or chamomile, leave the calendula out. Use plain sweet almond oil or jojoba oil instead.

12 months in a cool, dry cupboard. Keep the lid on when not in use to stop dust and skin oil getting in. Water-free balms like this one do not need to go in the fridge. Throw the balm out if it smells sharp, rancid, or paint-like. That means the sweet almond oil has gone off. Sweet almond oil keeps well, but any oil will turn if it gets warm.
